title: Merge official circuit priors from FIA/F1/Pirelli instead of inferring everything from raw laps
summary: Official sources encode real circuit asymmetry: mandatory dry-tyre rule state, compound durability differences, pre-race one-stop vs two-stop expectations, and track-specific pit-lane loss.
rationale: Official sources encode real circuit asymmetry: mandatory dry-tyre rule state, compound durability differences, pre-race one-stop vs two-stop expectations, and track-specific pit-lane loss. Those priors are not in the flat lap table but are directly relevant to pit pressure.
implementation_hint: Build a small lookup by `Race` or `Race_Year` with features like official pit-stop total-loss median from F1 pit-stop summaries, circuit severity bucket from Pirelli compound descriptions, and one-stop/two-stop prior from official strategy guides. Interact these with `LapsRemaining_Est`, `Compound`, `TyreLife`, and prior stop count.
expected_effect: Improves out-of-race generalization when identical lap features imply different strategy pressure on different circuits.
risk: Manual mapping effort and some priors are year-specific, so overfitting is possible if encoded too granularly.
